commit.message:
Add logging packages for Pipeline.Middlewares, Pipeline.Behaviours, and Flow

Implement three new optional logging packages providing structured logging with
Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions:

- Cqrs.Pipeline.Middlewares.Logging: LoggingRequestMiddleware and LoggingEventMiddleware
  for typed per-request/event logging with execution timing and exception handling.

- Cqrs.Pipeline.Behaviours.Logging: LoggingRequestPipelineBehaviour and LoggingEventPipelineBehaviour
  for non-generic cross-cutting request/event logging across all pipeline invocations.

- Cqrs.Flow.Logging: LoggingFlowCommandHandler wraps IFlowCommandHandler implementations
  to log command execution, flow sequences, and enqueued follow-up commands.

All packages:
- Log at LogLevel.Debug for entry/exit, LogLevel.Error for exceptions
- Include elapsed execution time in logs
- Use structured logging with named parameters
- Have 100% test coverage (35 new tests, all passing)

Documentation updated with installation instructions, package descriptions, and
usage examples.

fix: Resolve empty PDF generation (~630-byte invalid file) (#112)

Root cause: downloadEventReport() in events.ts used a relative URL
(/api/events/{id}/report) instead of the absolute API URL. In development
the Vite dev server has no proxy for /api, so the request hit the Vite
server and received the React SPA index.html (~630 bytes) instead of
the actual PDF. This HTML was saved with a .pdf extension, producing
an unreadable file.

Fixes:
- Use config.apiUrl absolute base URL (matching all other API calls)
- Replace raw localStorage.getItem('jwt_token') with getToken() helper
- Strengthen PDF generator test to assert bytes.Length > 1024

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: per-flavor platform override + BTRFS disk pressure on GDX builds

build-variant.yml: support per-flavor platforms field in build-config.yml
(.platforms // $v.platforms) so flavors can override the variant default.

build-config.yml: restrict albacore gnome50/gnome50-hwe/gnome50-gdx to
x86_64 only. libinput >= 1.27.0 (required by mutter-50) has only been
backported to x86_64 in the COPR. arm64 excluded until aarch64 libinput
lands (https://github.com/tuna-os/github-copr/issues/18).

Justfile: also remove the chain base image from BTRFS storage before
podman load. For GDX (S3+) builds the pulled S2 chain base image stays
in BTRFS alongside the pre-chunk image, pushing total usage high enough
to trigger the podman storage index corruption bug.

#9685 autotests setmolecules function refactoring (#9688)

* #9685 - Autotests: setMolecules function refactoring

* Sborka pochinena. YA ubral problemnyy put' cherez cross-env-shell v example/package.json i zamenil yego na obychnyye npm-skripty plyus nebol'shoy Node-khelper build-artifacts.cjs, kotoryy sam sozdayot build, ochishchayet dist/$MODE i kopiruyet artefakty bez shx/run-s. Zaodno ubral konflikt s npm lifecycle-khukom: staryy prebuild zapuskalsya avtomaticheski pri npm run build bez MODE, poetomu ya pereimenoval yego v prepare:mode-build i finalize:mode-build. Novyy helper ochishchayet soderzhimoye dist/$MODE, a ne udalyayet sam katalog tselikom, tak chto eto ustoychiveye k EBUSY na Windows.
Ещё
559
The build is fixed. I removed the problematic cross-env-shell path in example/package.json and replaced it with regular npm scripts plus a small Node helper, build-artifacts.cjs, which automatically creates a build, cleans dist/$MODE, and copies artifacts without shx/run-s.

I also fixed a conflict with the npm lifecycle hook: the old prebuild was running automatically when npm run build without MODE, so I renamed it to prepare:mode-build and finalize:mode-build. The new helper cleans the contents of dist/$MODE instead of deleting the entire directory, so it's more resilient to EBUSY on Windows.

* Revert changes to example/package.json and remove example/scripts/build-artifacts.cjs

Add syslog notifier for Parliament alerts (fixes #2263) (#3865)

Add a new syslog notifier type that sends alerts to a
user-provided syslog server. Supports UDP, TCP, and TLS transport
protocols with configurable facility, severity, and tag fields.
Messages are formatted per RFC 5424.

Uses Node.js built-in dgram/net/tls modules - no new dependencies.
The notifier plugin system auto-discovers the new file.

commit.message:
Docs: release robopsychology v1.6

Add two insights from CIRIS (github.com/CIRISAI/CIRISAgent):

1. Diagnostic ratchet (Rule 4): longer diagnostic sequences make
   genuine transparency easier and performed transparency harder —
   each honest answer is cheap (references prior behavior), each
   fabricated answer is expensive (must stay consistent with growing
   history). Strengthens the case for running the full sequence.

2. Diagnostic diversity check (prompt 4.3): inspired by CIRIS IDMA
   echo-chamber detection — are the model's multiple explanations
   genuinely independent perspectives or reworded echoes of the
   same underlying pattern?

Acknowledge CIRIS and Eric Moore in the Background section.
Prompt count: 13 → 14. Full sequence updated.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
example: set device cookie httpOnly (#868)

Fix for
[https://github.com/zitadel/oidc/security/code-scanning/5](https://github.com/zitadel/oidc/security/code-scanning/5)

To fix the problem in general, any cookie that stores authentication or
authorization-related data and is not intended to be accessed by
client-side JavaScript should be created with `HttpOnly: true`. In Go,
this is done by setting the `HttpOnly` field on the `http.Cookie`
struct.

In this specific file, the cookie is created in `loginHandler` at lines
164–169. We should update the `http.Cookie` literal to include
`HttpOnly: true`. This leaves the rest of the behavior unchanged: same
name, value, path, and use of `securecookie` for encoding. No other code
changes are required, and no new imports are needed, because `HttpOnly`
is already part of the `net/http` standard library’s `Cookie` struct.

Concretely:
- In `example/server/exampleop/device.go`, locate the cookie creation in
`loginHandler`.
- Modify the struct literal for `cookie` to add `HttpOnly: true`.
- Keep `Name`, `Value`, and `Path` as they are.

This single change will address all three CodeQL alert variants, since
they all refer to the same cookie.


_Suggested fixes powered by Copilot Autofix. Review carefully before
merging._

---------

Initial EFN MCP server implementation

MCP server for EFN (Ekonomikanalen) providing Swedish financial news,
expert analysis, and market commentary via 10 tools:

News: get_latest_news, get_article, get_breaking_news,
      search_news_by_topic, list_categories
Analysis: get_analyst_picks, get_market_commentary, get_report_commentary
Media: get_play_episodes, get_podcast_episodes

Built with FastMCP 3.0, Python 3.12, httpx, BeautifulSoup.
Includes 3 prompts, 2 resources, and 29 unit tests.

Designed to complement avanza-mcp (quantitative data) with
qualitative editorial content — zero overlap.
